text:
Once upon a quiet morning in the sleepy town of Everglade, there was a young boy named Oliver who had an unusual talent for flying. Unlike any bird, Oliver could glide gracefully through the air thanks to his enchanted flying boots.

Each morning after breakfast, Oliver would lace his boots, pat them down meticulously, and bounce softly onto his broomstick leaning against the shed at the edge of town. The townsfolk had grown accustomed to his morning rituals and sometimes joined him for a flyaround they'd call "Morning Glory."

One particularly beautiful Saturday, Oliver decided to host a flying exhibition for the kids in town. He dashed home, fluffed his feathers, and gathered a variety of colorful paper airplanes. He whispered charms into them as they took flight.

The exhibition consisted of soaring arcs, swift sharp turns, graceful circles, and even a few amusing dive bombs. Oliver invited his friends, Betty the bat, Rufus the raccoon, and even Mr. Finch who came proudly on his own
